babyLearning.en: growing rounder and stronger, learning the rhythm of your day
babyLearning.hi: gol aur mazboot hote hue, aapke din ki taal seekhte hue

title.hi: Mulya Jiye Jaate Hain, Sikhaaye Nahi

insight.en: Your child will not become who you tell them to be. They will become who they watch you be, day after quiet day.
insight.hi: Aapka bachcha woh nahi banega jo aap unhe banne ko kehti hain. Woh woh banega jo woh aapko har shaant din banta dekhta hai.

expanded.en: We often imagine raising a good person means giving the right speeches — explaining honesty, urging kindness, naming what matters. But a child learns values long before they understand words for them, by watching how we live. The way you keep a small promise, soften your voice when tired, or return a stranger's extra change — these are the real lessons. Long before your baby can be lectured, they will be quietly absorbing who you are. The good news is gentle: you do not have to be perfect. You only have to keep trying, in plain view, to live what you hope they will value.
expanded.hi: Hum aksar sochte hain ki ek achha insaan paalne ka matlab hai sahi bhaashan dena — imaandaari samjhaana, dayaalta ke liye kehna, jo maayne rakhta hai use naam dena. Par ek bachcha mulya us se bahut pehle seekh leta hai jab woh unke shabd samajhta hai, yeh dekhkar ki hum kaise jeete hain. Jis tarah aap ek chhota vaada nibhaati hain, thaki hui aawaz naram karti hain, ya kisi ajnabi ka extra change lauta deti hain — yahi asli sabak hain. Baby ko bhaashan diye jaane se bahut pehle, woh chupchaap yeh seekh raha hoga ki aap kaun hain. Achhi khabar narm hai: aapko perfect hone ki zaroorat nahi. Aapko bas saamne, koshish karte rehna hai, jo aap chahti hain ki woh maane use jeene ki.

deepDive.en: Developmental research shows children imitate observed behaviour far more reliably than spoken instruction — a pattern psychologists trace back to early modelling. What you do teaches louder than what you say.
deepDive.hi: Developmental research dikhaati hai ki bachche dekhe gaye vyavhaar ki nakal kahin zyada bharose se karte hain, bole gaye nirdesh se kam — ek pattern jise psychologists shuruaati modelling tak le jaate hain. Aap jo karti hain woh aapke kehne se zyada zor se sikhaata hai.

remember.en: Children become what they watch, not what they are told.
remember.hi: Bachche woh bante hain jo woh dekhte hain, na ki jo unhe kaha jaata hai.

title.hi: Bhed Ki Khaal Mein Bhediya

summary.en: A wolf hides its true nature in a disguise, but a borrowed skin cannot change what lies beneath.
summary.hi: Ek bhediya apni asli fitrat ko bhes mein chhupaata hai, par udhaar li gayi khaal andar ki cheez nahi badal sakti.

body.en:
A hungry wolf had been chased away from a flock too many times by the watchful shepherd and his dogs. One day, he found the skin of a sheep lying near the field. A clever idea came to him. He pulled the soft fleece over himself, lowered his head, and walked quietly in among the grazing sheep, who did not notice the stranger in their midst.

For a while the trick worked, and the wolf grinned beneath his disguise. But that very evening, the shepherd decided he wanted meat for his supper. He reached into the flock for the plumpest-looking sheep — and his hand closed upon the wolf wearing the fleece.

In the dark, by his own choice of disguise, the wolf was taken for an ordinary sheep and met the very end he had schemed to escape. A costume may fool the eye for a time, but it never truly changes what we are within. Who we are has a way of finding us out.
body.hi:
Ek bhookha bhediya chaukanne charvaahe aur uske kutton dwara jhund se kai baar bhagaya ja chuka tha. Ek din, use khet ke paas padi ek bhed ki khaal mili. Use ek chalaak vichaar aaya. Usne naram oon ko khud par chadha liya, sir niche kiya, aur charti hui bhedon ke beech chupchaap chal pada, jinhone apne beech ajnabi ko note nahi kiya.

Thodi der yeh chaal chali, aur bhediya apne bhes ke niche muskuraaya. Par usi shaam, charvaahe ne socha ki use raat ke khaane ke liye gosht chahiye. Usne jhund mein se sabse mote dikhne wali bhed ke liye haath badhaya — aur uska haath oon pehne bhediye par ja pada.

Andhere mein, apne hi chune hue bhes se, bhediya ek aam bhed samjha gaya aur usi anjaam tak pohancha jisse bachne ki usne saazish ki thi. Ek bhes thodi der aankh ko bewakoof bana sakta hai, par yeh kabhi sach mein nahi badalta ki hum andar se kya hain. Hum kaun hain, yeh khud humein dhoondh hi leta hai.

title.en: Tell your baby why honesty matters so much to you, and where you first learned its worth.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki imaandaari aapke liye itni kyun maayne rakhti hai, aur aapne uski keemat pehli baar kahan seekhi.

motivation.en: When you name a value out loud, you begin to live it more clearly for them.
motivation.hi: Jab aap ek mulya ko bolkar naam deti hain, aap use unke liye aur saaf jeena shuru karti hain.

title.hi: Woh Insaan Jo Main Ban Rahi Hoon

description.en: A short meditation on the self your baby will know
description.hi: Us khud par ek chhota dhyan jise aapka baby jaanega

affirmation.en: The qualities I gently grow in myself today are the first gifts I hand to you.
affirmation.hi: Jo gun main aaj khud mein pyaar se badhaati hoon, wahi pehle tohfe hain jo main tumhe deti hoon.

introduction.en: Settle comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths and feel your body grow still. Bring to mind one quality you most hope your baby will carry — honesty, kindness, courage. Picture yourself living it gently today, and let that picture warm you.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein aur apne sharir ko shaant hote mehsoos karein. Ek gun yaad karein jo aap sabse zyada chahti hain ki aapka baby rakhe — imaandaari, dayaalta, himmat. Khud ko aaj use pyaar se jeete hue sochein, aur us tasveer ko apne andar garm hone dein.

closingThought.en: You do not have to teach your baby this quality with words. You are already showing it, simply by becoming it.
closingThought.hi: Aapko apne baby ko yeh gun shabdon se sikhane ki zaroorat nahi. Aap pehle se hi use dikha rahi hain, bas use bankar.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.en: I Lead By Who I Am
title.hi: Main Apne Hone Se Raah Dikhaati Hoon

content.en: I do not need perfect words to raise a good person. I only need to keep living, gently, the values I hope to pass on.
content.hi: Ek achha insaan paalne ke liye mujhe perfect shabd nahi chahiye. Mujhe bas, pyaar se, woh mulya jeete rehne hain jo main aage dena chahti hoon.

remember.en: Your everyday self is your child's first teacher.
remember.hi: Aapka rozana khud aapke bachche ka pehla guru hai.

babyLearning.en: settling into a snug fit, kicking with new strength
babyLearning.hi: ek aaramdayak jagah mein bas raha hai, nayi taakat se laat maar raha hai

title.hi: Imaandaari Ghar Se Shuru Hoti Hai

insight.en: A child learns truth-telling not from rules about lying, but from a home where honesty feels safe.
insight.hi: Ek bachcha sach bolna jhooth ke niyamon se nahi seekhta, balki ek aise ghar se jahan imaandaari surakshit lagti hai.

expanded.en: We often think honesty is taught by punishing lies. But children tell the truth most easily where the truth is welcomed gently — where a small confession is met with calm rather than anger. The home you are building will be your baby's first place to practise being honest. If mistakes can be admitted without fear, and feelings can be spoken without shame, your child learns that truth and love can live together. You begin this now, in how openly you speak, how kindly you respond, how honestly you let yourself be seen. Honesty is less a rule you enforce than an air your child gets to breathe.
expanded.hi: Hum aksar sochte hain ki imaandaari jhooth ko saza dekar sikhaayi jaati hai. Par bachche sach wahan sabse aasaani se bolte hain jahan sach ka narmi se swagat hota hai — jahan ek chhoti se baat manne par gusse ke bajaaye shaanti milti hai. Jo ghar aap bana rahi hain woh aapke baby ki imaandaar hone ka pehla abhyaas-sthal hoga. Agar galtiyaan bina dar ke maani ja sakein, aur bhaavnaayein bina sharm ke kahi ja sakein, to aapka bachcha seekhta hai ki sach aur pyaar saath reh sakte hain. Aap yeh abhi se shuru karti hain, is mein ki aap kitne khulkar bolti hain, kitni dayaalta se jawaab deti hain, kitni imaandaari se khud ko dekhne deti hain. Imaandaari ek niyam se zyada ek hawa hai jise aapka bachcha saans le paata hai.

deepDive.en: Studies on children and honesty find that warmth and safety encourage truth-telling, while fear of harsh punishment teaches children to hide. A gentle home grows an honest child.
deepDive.hi: Bachchon aur imaandaari par hui studies paati hain ki garmjoshi aur surakshit ehsaas sach bolne ko badhaate hain, jabki kathor saza ka dar bachchon ko chhupna sikhaata hai. Ek narm ghar ek imaandaar bachcha badhaata hai.

remember.en: Truth grows best where it is safe to tell.
remember.hi: Sach wahan sabse achha badhta hai jahan use kehna surakshit ho.

title.en: The Sparrow And The King's Justice
title.hi: Gauraiya Aur Raja Ka Nyay

summary.en: A tiny sparrow asks a great king for justice, and a wise ruler proves no truth is too small to honour.
summary.hi: Ek nanhi gauraiya ek bade raja se nyay maangti hai, aur ek samajhdaar shaasak saabit karta hai ki koi sach itna chhota nahi ki uska samman na ho.

body.en:
A pair of sparrows had built their nest in the eaves of a king's grand hall, and there the mother sparrow laid her tiny eggs. One morning a careless elephant from the royal stables brushed against the wall, and the little nest tumbled down, breaking the eggs upon the stone floor.

The grieving sparrow did not fly away in silence. She flew straight up to the king's throne and chirped her complaint boldly. The courtiers laughed — what was a sparrow's nest to a mighty king? But the king raised his hand for quiet. "A wrong is a wrong," he said, "whether it is done to the great or the small. Justice that ignores the weak is no justice at all."

He ordered the elephant-keeper to be more careful, and had a safe new ledge built for the sparrows. The tiny bird flew home content. From that day the people loved their king all the more — for they saw that he would weigh the truth of even the smallest creature with the same fair heart.
body.hi:
Ek gauraiya jode ne ek raja ke shaandaar mahal ki chhajli mein apna ghonsla banaya tha, aur wahan maa gauraiya ne apne nanhe ande diye. Ek subah shaahi astabal ka ek laaparwah haathi deewar se ragad gaya, aur nanha ghonsla niche gir pada, patthar ke farsh par ande toot gaye.

Dukhi gauraiya chupchaap udd nahi gayi. Woh seedhe raja ke sinhaasan tak udi aur himmat se apni shikaayat chahchaha di. Darbaariyon ne hansa — ek bade raja ke aage gauraiya ke ghonsle ki kya keemat? Par raja ne shaanti ke liye haath uthaaya. "Galat galat hi hai," usne kaha, "chaahe woh bade ke saath ho ya chhote ke saath. Jo nyay kamzor ko andekha kare woh nyay hai hi nahi."

Usne haathi ke rakhwaale ko zyada savdhaan rehne ka hukm diya, aur gauraiyon ke liye ek surakshit nayi chhajli banwaayi. Nanhi chidiya santusht ghar udi. Us din se log apne raja ko aur bhi zyada chaahne lage — kyunki unhone dekha ki woh sabse chhote jeev ke sach ko bhi usi nyaaypoorn dil se taulega.

title.en: Tell your baby about the kind of gentleness you most hope to model for them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us tarah ki narmi ke baare mein bataayein jo aap unke liye sabse zyada banne ki ummeed karti hain.

motivation.en: The kindness you describe today becomes the kindness they feel from you tomorrow.
motivation.hi: Jo dayaalta aap aaj bataati hain, woh kal woh dayaalta ban jaati hai jo woh aapse mehsoos karte hain.

title.en: Our Home Is A Safe Place For Truth
title.hi: Hamara Ghar Sach Ke Liye Surakshit Jagah Hai

affirmation.en: I am building a home where you can always be honest, where truth is met with love and never with fear.
affirmation.hi: Main ek aisa ghar bana rahi hoon jahan tum hamesha imaandaar reh sako, jahan sach ko pyaar mile, dar kabhi nahi.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.hi: Pet Mein Saans Bharein

content.en: Rest a hand low on your belly. Breathe in slowly and feel it rise gently under your palm, then let it fall as you breathe out. Stay with five soft, rounded breaths.
content.hi: Ek haath pet par niche rakhein. Dheere saans lein aur use apni hatheli ke niche naram tarah uthte mehsoos karein, phir saans chhodte hue use girne dein. Paanch naram, gol saanson ke saath rahein.

remember.en: Your slow breath is a calm your baby shares.
remember.hi: Aapki dheemi saans ek shaanti hai jo aapka baby baantta hai.

babyLearning.en: opening and closing tiny eyes, sensing soft light
babyLearning.hi: nanhi aankhein kholte aur band karte, naram roshni mehsoos karte

title.hi: Dayaalta Dayaalta Se Sikhaayi Jaati Hai

insight.en: A child does not learn to be kind from being told to share. They learn it from being treated kindly first.
insight.hi: Ek bachcha dayaalu hona baant ne ko kahe jaane se nahi seekhta. Woh ise pehle khud ke saath dayaalta se pesh aane se seekhta hai.

expanded.en: Kindness is not a lesson we deliver; it is a warmth we pass on. A child who is met with patience when small, comforted when upset, and spoken to gently learns in their very body what kindness feels like — and then has it to give. You can urge a child to be nice a thousand times, but it is the kindness they receive that truly teaches. As you prepare to meet your baby, the tenderness you offer will not spoil them. It will fill them, until kindness becomes their first and most natural language. What we pour into a child is what eventually pours out.
expanded.hi: Dayaalta koi sabak nahi jo hum dete hain; yeh ek garmjoshi hai jo hum aage badhaate hain. Ek bachcha jise chhota hone par sabra mila, pareshaan hone par dilaasa mila, aur narmi se baat ki gayi, woh apne sharir mein hi seekhta hai ki dayaalta kaisi lagti hai — aur phir use dene ke liye uske paas hoti hai. Aap ek bachche ko hazaar baar achha banne ko keh sakti hain, par sach mein wahi dayaalta sikhaati hai jo unhe milti hai. Jaise aap apne baby se milne ki taiyaari karti hain, jo komalta aap dengi woh unhe bigaadegi nahi. Woh unhe bharegi, jab tak dayaalta unki pehli aur sabse swaabhaavik bhaasha na ban jaaye. Hum ek bachche mein jo bharte hain wahi aakhir mein bahar nikalta hai.

deepDive.en: Research on empathy finds children who experience warm, responsive care show more compassion toward others. Kindness received in early life becomes kindness expressed later.
deepDive.hi: Empathy par hui research paati hai ki jin bachchon ko garmjoshi bhari, responsive dekhbhaal milti hai woh doosron ke prati zyada karuna dikhaate hain. Shuruaati jeevan mein mili dayaalta baad mein zaahir ki gayi dayaalta ban jaati hai.

remember.en: The kindness a child receives is the kindness they give.
remember.hi: Jo dayaalta ek bachche ko milti hai wahi woh deta hai.

title.hi: Raja Harishchandra Ka Vaada

summary.en: A king gives away everything he owns rather than break his word, proving truth is worth more than a kingdom.
summary.hi: Ek raja apna sab kuch de deta hai par apna vaada nahi todta, yeh saabit karte hue ki sach ek raajya se zyada keemti hai.

body.en:
King Harishchandra was famed across the land for one rare quality: he never spoke a word that was not true, and he never broke a promise once given. To test him, a great sage came to him and reminded him of a pledge the king had once made in a dream — to give away all that he owned.

Though it cost him dearly, the king did not argue or excuse himself. He handed over his kingdom, his palace, even his royal robes, keeping nothing back. To pay what was still owed, he gave up his comfort and worked humbly at the lowest tasks, his queen and small son beside him, never once complaining that the price was too high.

Through every hardship, Harishchandra held fast to his word. In the end, his unbroken honesty so moved the heavens that all he had lost was returned to him many times over. His name is remembered to this day as a promise kept whatever the cost — for a word truly meant is a treasure greater than any crown.
body.hi:
Raja Harishchandra poori dharti mein ek durlabh gun ke liye mashhoor the: woh kabhi koi aisa shabd nahi bolte the jo sach na ho, aur ek baar diya vaada kabhi nahi todte the. Unhe parakhne ke liye, ek mahaan rishi unke paas aaye aur unhe ek vaada yaad dilaya jo raja ne kabhi sapne mein kiya tha — apna sab kuch de dene ka.

Haalanki yeh unhe bahut mehnga pada, raja ne behes nahi ki na koi bahaana banaya. Unhone apna raajya, apna mahal, yahaan tak ki apne raajsi vastra bhi de diye, kuch nahi bachaya. Jo abhi baaki tha use chukane ke liye, unhone apna aaram tyaag diya aur sabse niche ke kaamon mein vinamrata se kaam kiya, apni raani aur chhote bete ke saath, ek baar bhi shikaayat nahi ki ki keemat bahut zyada hai.

Har mushkil mein, Harishchandra apne shabd par dridh rahe. Aakhir mein, unki na tooti imaandaari ne aasmaan ko itna chhuaa ki jo kuch unhone khoya tha woh unhe kai gunaa wapas mila. Unka naam aaj tak ek aise vaade ke roop mein yaad kiya jaata hai jo har keemat par nibhaaya gaya — kyunki sach mein nibhaaya gaya ek shabd kisi bhi taaj se bada khazaana hai.

title.en: Tell your baby how you hope to show them respect, even when they are very small.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap unhe samman kaise dikhana chahti hain, tab bhi jab woh bahut chhote ho.

motivation.en: Children learn to respect others by first being respected themselves.
motivation.hi: Bachche doosron ka samman karna pehle khud ka samman paakar seekhte hain.

title.hi: Raag Gorakh Kalyan

description.en: Evening raga for a tender, settling calm
description.hi: Shaam ka raag, ek komal, thamti shaanti ke liye

affirmation.en: As this evening melody softens the air, a gentle steadiness settles in me, and in you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh shaam ki dhun hawa ko naram karti hai, ek komal sthirta mujhmein, aur tumme, bas jaati hai.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.hi: Jab Khaane Ke Baad Bhaaripan Rehta Hai

content.en: As your baby grows, there is less room for a full stomach, and big meals can leave you heavy and uncomfortable. Try eating smaller portions more often through the day, and sip warm water rather than gulping it down. A short, gentle walk after eating helps your body settle.
content.hi: Jaise aapka baby badhta hai, bhare pet ke liye kam jagah hoti hai, aur bade khaane aapko bhaari aur asahaj chhod sakte hain. Din bhar mein thodi-thodi maatra mein zyada baar khaane ki koshish karein, aur paani gatakne ke bajaaye garm paani ki chuski lein. Khaane ke baad ek chhoti, naram sair aapke sharir ko thamne mein madad karti hai.

remember.en: Smaller, gentler meals sit easier as your baby grows.
remember.hi: Chhote, narm khaane aapke baby ke badhne ke saath aasaani se rehte hain.

babyLearning.en: practising tiny breaths, getting ready bit by bit
babyLearning.hi: nanhi saansein abhyaas karte, thoda-thoda taiyaar hote

title.hi: Jo Aap Sehte Hain, Wahi Sikhaate Hain

insight.en: Children watch not only what you praise, but what you let pass. Both quietly teach them what is okay.
insight.hi: Bachche sirf woh nahi dekhte jiski aap taareef karti hain, balki woh bhi jise aap jaane deti hain. Dono chupchaap unhe sikhaate hain ki kya theek hai.

expanded.en: We teach our children with our words, but we teach them just as deeply with what we accept without a word. The unkindness we laugh off, the small dishonesty we shrug at, the disrespect we let slide — a watching child learns from these too. This is not about being harsh or rigid. It is about noticing that your gentle 'no' to what is wrong, and your quiet steadiness around what matters, are lessons in themselves. You are always teaching, even in silence. Choosing what you will and will not tolerate, kindly and clearly, is one of the quietest ways you shape a child's sense of right.
expanded.hi: Hum apne bachchon ko apne shabdon se sikhaate hain, par hum unhe utni hi gehraai se us se bhi sikhaate hain jise hum bina kuch kahe sweekaar kar lete hain. Jis rookhepan par hum hans dete hain, jis chhoti beimaani par hum kandhe uchkaate hain, jis anaadar ko hum jaane dete hain — ek dekhta bachcha in se bhi seekhta hai. Yeh kathor ya akkad hone ke baare mein nahi hai. Yeh yeh note karne ke baare mein hai ki galat cheez ko aapka narm 'naa,' aur jo maayne rakhta hai uske aaspaas aapki shaant sthirta, khud mein sabak hain. Aap hamesha sikhaa rahi hain, chuppi mein bhi. Yeh chunna ki aap kya sahengi aur kya nahi, dayaalta aur saafgoi se, ek bachche ke sahi ke ehsaas ko gadhne ke sabse shaant tareekon mein se ek hai.

deepDive.en: Psychologists note that children read inconsistency keenly — what adults allow carries as much weight as what they say. Quiet, consistent boundaries teach values more reliably than lectures.
deepDive.hi: Psychologists note karte hain ki bachche asangati ko tezi se padhte hain — jo bade allow karte hain woh utna hi vajan rakhta hai jitna woh kehte hain. Shaant, sthir seemaayein bhaashan se zyada bharose se mulya sikhaati hain.

remember.en: What you quietly accept teaches as loudly as what you say.
remember.hi: Jise aap chupchaap sweekaar karti hain woh utni hi zor se sikhaata hai jitna aapka kehna.

title.en: The Honest Merchant And The Greedy One
title.hi: Imaandaar Vyapaari Aur Laalchi Vyapaari

summary.en: Two merchants split a town, and the honest dealer's fair name outlasts the greedy one's cheap tricks.
summary.hi: Do vyapaari ek shahar baant lete hain, aur imaandaar vyapaari ka nyaaypoorn naam laalchi ke saste hathkando se zyada tikta hai.

body.en:
Two merchants traded in the same town, and to avoid quarrels they agreed to share it — one would sell on the eastern streets, the other on the western. The first merchant was honest. He weighed his goods truly, named a fair price, and never deceived a customer, even when he might have gained.

The second was greedy. He shaved his weights, mixed cheap grain with good, and sweet-talked buyers into paying too much. For a while his purse filled faster, and he laughed at his honest rival as a fool.

But word travels. Soon the townsfolk learned whose scales were true and whose were crooked. They walked the extra distance to buy from the honest merchant, trusting him with their custom and their friendship. The greedy man's stalls grew empty while the honest man's prospered. In time, all the trade came to the one whose word could be believed. A fair name, slowly earned, is worth more than any quick coin won by a trick.
body.hi:
Do vyapaari ek hi shahar mein vyapaar karte the, aur jhagdon se bachne ke liye unhone use baant ne par sehmati ban li — ek poorvi galiyon mein bechega, doosra pashchimi mein. Pehla vyapaari imaandaar tha. Woh apna saamaan sach mein taulta, ek nyaaypoorn keemat bataata, aur kisi grahak ko kabhi dhokha nahi deta, tab bhi jab use faayda ho sakta.

Doosra laalchi tha. Woh apne baat kam karta, achhe anaaj mein sasta milaata, aur khareedaaron ko meethi baaton se zyada paise dene par phusla leta. Thodi der uska bataua jaldi bharta, aur woh apne imaandaar pratidwandi par bewakoof keh kar hansta.

Par baat failti hai. Jald hi shahar ke logon ne jaan liya ki kiski taraazu sachi hai aur kiski tedhi. Woh imaandaar vyapaari se khareedne ke liye thoda zyada chalte, use apna vyapaar aur apni dosti sompte. Laalchi aadmi ki dukaanein khaali hone lagi jabki imaandaar aadmi ki phalti-phoolti rahi. Samay ke saath, saara vyapaar usi ke paas aa gaya jiske shabd par yaqeen kiya ja sakta tha. Ek nyaaypoorn naam, dheere kamaaya gaya, kisi chaal se jeete jhatpat sikke se zyada keemti hai.

title.en: Tell your baby why you value effort and honest hard work, and a time it carried you.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap mehnat aur imaandaar parishram ki kadar kyun karti hain, aur ek samay jab isne aapko thaama.

motivation.en: Children come to love what they see their parents honour with patience.
motivation.hi: Bachche us cheez se pyaar karne lagte hain jise woh apne parents ko sabra se samman dete dekhte hain.

title.hi: Beech Mein Sthir

description.en: A short meditation to find your quiet steadiness
description.hi: Apni shaant sthirta paane ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: Whatever moves around me, I can return to a calm, steady centre — and you rest there with me.
affirmation.hi: Mere aaspaas chaahe jo hile, main ek shaant, sthir beech mein laut sakti hoon — aur tum wahan mere saath aaram karte ho.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths, letting the body grow heavy and still. Imagine a calm, steady point deep in your centre, like the quiet heart of a spinning wheel. With each breath, settle a little more gently into it.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein aur aankhein band hone dein. Teen dheemi saansein lein, sharir ko bhaari aur shaant hone dete hue. Apne beech mein gehre ek shaant, sthir bindu sochein, ek ghoomte pahiye ke shaant dil ki tarah. Har saans ke saath, usme thodi aur narmi se bas jaayein.

closingThought.en: This steady centre is always within you. When days feel busy, your baby feels your calm return to it.
closingThought.hi: Yeh sthir beech hamesha aapke andar hai. Jab din vyast lagein, aapka baby aapki shaanti ko usme lautte mehsoos karta hai.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Lambi Saans Bahar

content.en: Breathe in gently for a count of four, then let the breath flow out slowly for a count of six or seven. The longer out-breath signals your body to soften. Repeat for five rounds.
content.hi: Chaar tak ginte hue narmi se saans lein, phir chhah ya saat tak ginte hue saans ko dheere bahar behne dein. Lambi bahar wali saans aapke sharir ko naram hone ka ishaara deti hai. Paanch baar dohraayein.

remember.en: A slow exhale tells the body it is safe to rest.
remember.hi: Ek dheemi saans bahar sharir ko batati hai ki aaram karna surakshit hai.

babyLearning.en: filling out softly, fingers and toes complete
babyLearning.hi: naram tarah bharte hue, ungliyaan aur paer poore

title.hi: Chhoti Cheezon Mein Imaandaari

insight.en: Character is not built in big, dramatic choices. It is built in the tiny ones no one is watching.
insight.hi: Charitra bade, naataki faislon mein nahi banta. Yeh un chhote faislon mein banta hai jinhe koi nahi dekh raha.

expanded.en: We often think integrity is about rising to grand occasions. But the truer test is in the small, unseen moments: returning a borrowed thing on time, telling the cashier they gave too much change, keeping a promise no one would have checked. Children have a remarkable eye for these little consistencies. They notice when our words match our quiet actions, and when they do not. By being honest in the small things — not for praise, but simply because it is who you are — you teach your child that integrity is not a performance for others. It is a steady way of being, kept even when no one would ever know.
expanded.hi: Hum aksar sochte hain ki imaandaari bade mauqon par khade hone ke baare mein hai. Par sachi pariksha chhote, andekhe palon mein hoti hai: udhaar li cheez samay par lautana, cashier ko batana ki unhone zyada change diya, ek aisa vaada nibhaana jise koi check bhi nahi karta. Bachchon ki in chhoti sangatiyon par anokhi nazar hoti hai. Woh note karte hain jab hamare shabd hamare shaant kaamon se milte hain, aur jab nahi milte. Chhoti cheezon mein imaandaar rehkar — taareef ke liye nahi, balki bas isliye ki yeh aap hain — aap apne bachche ko sikhaati hain ki imaandaari doosron ke liye ek pradarshan nahi hai. Yeh ek sthir tarah ka hona hai, tab bhi nibhaaya gaya jab kisi ko kabhi pata na chalta.

deepDive.en: Ethics researchers find that integrity practised in everyday small acts is what most strongly shapes a child's moral compass — far more than occasional grand gestures.
deepDive.hi: Ethics ke researchers paate hain ki rozana ke chhote kaamon mein nibhaayi gayi imaandaari hi ek bachche ke naitik disha-bodh ko sabse zyada gadhti hai — kabhi-kabhaar ke bade ishaaron se kahin zyada.

remember.en: Who you are when no one watches is what your child learns.
remember.hi: Jab koi nahi dekh raha hota tab aap kaun hain, wahi aapka bachcha seekhta hai.

title.en: Tenali Rama And The Honest Thief
title.hi: Tenali Rama Aur Imaandaar Chor

summary.en: Clever Tenali Rama turns a thief's own honest moment into the wisest of lessons.
summary.hi: Chalaak Tenali Rama ek chor ke apne imaandaar pal ko sabse samajhdaar sabak mein badal deta hai.

body.en:
One night a thief crept into Tenali Rama's garden, hoping to steal from the famous poet's home. Tenali, awake and clever as ever, guessed at once what was afoot. So he whispered loudly to his wife, "I hear thieves about. Let us hide all our gold in the old chest and lower it into the well, where no robber will think to look."

The listening thief grinned, slipped into the well that night, and hauled up the heavy chest. But when he forced it open, it held only stones. Furious yet curious, he came back the next day to confront Tenali.

"Why did you trick me?" the thief demanded. Tenali smiled. "You worked so hard hauling that chest — and you have watered my whole garden doing it, for the well-rope ran through my fields. An honest day's labour suits you far better than stealing." The thief, shamed and strangely moved, laughed at himself and went away resolved to earn his bread honestly from that day on.
body.hi:
Ek raat ek chor Tenali Rama ke bageeche mein ghusa, mashhoor kavi ke ghar se churaane ki ummeed mein. Tenali, jaaga hua aur hamesha ki tarah chalaak, turant samajh gaya ki kya chal raha hai. To usne apni patni se zor se kaanaphoosi ki, "Mujhe chor aaspaas lag rahe hain. Chalo apna saara sona purane sandook mein chhupaa kar use kuein mein utaar dein, jahan koi luttera dekhne ki nahi sochega."

Sunta chor muskuraaya, us raat kuein mein utra, aur bhaari sandook upar kheencha. Par jab usne use zabardasti khola, usme sirf patthar the. Gusse mein par utsuk, woh agle din Tenali se bhidne wapas aaya.

"Tumne mujhe kyun thaga?" chor ne poocha. Tenali muskuraaya. "Tumne us sandook ko kheenchne mein itni mehnat ki — aur aisa karte hue tumne mera poora bageecha seench diya, kyunki kuein ki rassi mere kheton se hokar guzarti hai. Ek imaandaar din ki mehnat tumpe chori se kahin behtar suhaati hai." Chor, sharminda aur ajeeb tarah se chhua hua, khud par hansa aur us din se apni roti imaandaari se kamane ka pran lekar chala gaya.

title.en: Tell your baby about the generosity you most want them to see in you.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us udaarta ke baare mein bataayein jo aap sabse zyada chahti hain ki woh aapme dekhe.

motivation.en: Children grow generous hearts by living among generous hands.
motivation.hi: Bachche udaar dilon ke saath badhte hain udaar haathon ke beech rehkar.

title.en: I Am True In The Smallest Things
title.hi: Main Sabse Chhoti Cheezon Mein Sachi Hoon

affirmation.en: I keep my word in the quiet, unseen moments, so that honesty becomes the air you will grow up breathing.
affirmation.hi: Main shaant, andekhe palon mein apna shabd nibhaati hoon, taaki imaandaari woh hawa ban jaaye jismein tum bade hoge.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.en: When Acidity Creeps Up At Night
title.hi: Jab Raat Mein Acidity Badhti Hai

content.en: In the third trimester, the rising belly can push stomach acid upward, leaving a burning feeling, especially when you lie down. Try to finish your last meal a couple of hours before bed, keep it light, and avoid very spicy or fried food in the evening. Resting propped up on an extra pillow can ease it.
content.hi: Teesri trimester mein, badhta pet pet ke acid ko upar dhakel sakta hai, jisse jalan ka ehsaas hota hai, khaaskar jab aap letti hain. Apna aakhri khaana sone se kuch ghante pehle khatam karne ki koshish karein, use halka rakhein, aur shaam ko bahut teekha ya tala hua khaana na khaayein. Ek extra takiye par tek lagaakar aaram karna ise kam kar sakta hai.

remember.en: A light, early dinner sits gently through the night.
remember.hi: Ek halka, jaldi ka dinner raat bhar narmi se rehta hai.

babyLearning.en: responding to your voice, calmed by its sound
babyLearning.hi: aapki aawaz ka jawaab dete, uski awaaz se shaant hote

title.en: Generosity Is Learned At The Table
title.hi: Udaarta Mez Par Seekhi Jaati Hai

insight.en: A child learns to share not from being told to, but from sitting where giving is simply how the family lives.
insight.hi: Ek bachcha baant na kahe jaane se nahi seekhta, balki wahan baithkar jahan dena bas yahi hai ki parivar kaise jeeta hai.

expanded.en: Some of the deepest lessons in generosity are taught at the family table — the extra plate kept for a guest, food shared without counting, the gentle 'take some more' offered to whoever is near. A child raised where giving is ordinary, not occasional, learns that abundance is something we pass around, not hoard. You do not need grand acts of charity to teach this. The everyday warmth of sharing a meal, welcoming someone in, offering the best piece to another — these small, repeated gestures teach a child that to have is to be able to give. Generosity learned this young becomes simply how they meet the world.
expanded.hi: Udaarta ke kuch sabse gehre sabak parivar ki mez par sikhaaye jaate hain — mehmaan ke liye rakhi gayi ek extra plate, bina gine baanta gaya khaana, jo bhi paas ho use diya gaya naram 'aur lo.' Ek bachcha jahan dena aam hai, kabhi-kabhaar nahi, woh seekhta hai ki prachurta woh cheez hai jise hum baantte hain, jamaa nahi karte. Yeh sikhane ke liye aapko daan ke bade kaam nahi chahiye. Khaana baant ne, kisi ka swagat karne, doosre ko sabse achha tukda dene ki rozana garmjoshi — yeh chhote, baar-baar hone wale ishaare ek bachche ko sikhaate hain ki paana yaani de paana. Itni chhoti umar mein seekhi udaarta bas yahi ban jaati hai ki woh duniya se kaise milte hain.

deepDive.en: Studies on prosocial behaviour suggest children become more giving when generosity is a visible, everyday family norm rather than an occasional taught lesson. They copy what they live around.
deepDive.hi: Prosocial vyavhaar par hui studies sujhaati hain ki bachche tab zyada dene wale bante hain jab udaarta ek dikhne wala, rozana ka parivaarik niyam ho na ki kabhi-kabhaar sikhaaya gaya sabak. Woh us cheez ki nakal karte hain jiske beech jeete hain.

remember.en: Where giving is ordinary, a giving child grows.
remember.hi: Jahan dena aam hai, wahan ek dene wala bachcha badhta hai.

title.hi: Woh Raja Jise Sona Pyaara Tha

summary.en: A king who hoards gold above all learns too late that a heart of gold is the only real treasure.
summary.hi: Ek raja jise sab se badhkar sona jamaa karna pyaara hai, der se seekhta hai ki sone ka dil hi ekmaatra asli khazaana hai.

body.en:
There once lived a king who loved gold above all else. He counted his coins each night, locked his treasure behind heavy doors, and turned away beggars at his gate, fearing every gift would lessen his hoard. "Gold is the only thing that lasts," he would say, and his halls grew rich, but cold and silent.

One harsh winter, a great famine swept the land. The people had no grain, and the king's storehouses overflowed. Yet still he would not open them, clutching his gold as the fields lay bare. His own subjects began to leave, seeking kinder lands, until his glittering palace stood nearly empty.

Alone among his locked chests, the king at last understood. Gold could not be eaten, could not keep him company, could not buy back the love he had turned away. With trembling hands he flung open his storehouses and his treasury, feeding all who remained. The joy that returned to his halls taught him what no coin ever had: that what we give away warms us far longer than what we keep.
body.hi:
Ek baar ek raja rehta tha jise sab se badhkar sona pyaara tha. Woh har raat apne sikke ginta, apna khazaana bhaari darwaazon ke peeche taala lagaata, aur apne darwaaze par bhikhaariyon ko lauta deta, is dar se ki har daan uska jamaa ghata dega. "Sona hi ekmaatra cheez hai jo tikti hai," woh kehta, aur uske mahal ameer ho gaye, par thande aur khaamosh.

Ek kathor sardi mein, ek bada akaal dharti par chha gaya. Logon ke paas anaaj nahi tha, aur raja ke godaam chhalak rahe the. Phir bhi woh unhe nahi kholta, apna sona pakde rehta jab khet ujad pade the. Uski apni praja jaane lagi, dayaalu zameenon ki talaash mein, jab tak uska chamakta mahal lagbhag khaali na ho gaya.

Apne taale lage sandookon ke beech akela, raja ko aakhir samajh aaya. Sona khaaya nahi ja sakta tha, uska saath nahi de sakta tha, us pyaar ko wapas nahi khareed sakta tha jise usne lauta diya tha. Kaanpte haathon se usne apne godaam aur khazaana khol diya, jo bhi bache the sab ko khilaaya. Jo khushi uske mahal mein lauti usne use woh sikhaaya jo kisi sikke ne kabhi nahi: ki jo hum de dete hain woh humein us se kahin zyada der garm rakhta hai jo hum rakhte hain.

title.en: Tell your baby how you hope to own your mistakes openly in front of them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap unke saamne apni galtiyaan khulkar kaise maanna chahti hain.

motivation.en: A child who sees a parent admit fault learns that honesty is braver than pretending.
motivation.hi: Ek bachcha jo parent ko galti maante dekhta hai woh seekhta hai ki imaandaari dikhaawa karne se zyada himmat wali hai.

title.hi: Raag Pahadi

description.en: Evening raga for a warm, open-hearted calm
description.hi: Shaam ka raag, ek garm, khule-dil wali shaanti ke liye

affirmation.en: As this gentle hill melody rises and falls, my heart opens softly, holding you within it.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh komal pahaadi dhun uthti aur girti hai, mera dil narmi se khulta hai, tumhe apne andar thaame.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.hi: Mere Paas Baantne Ko Kaafi Hai

content.en: I do not give from emptiness, but from a quiet fullness. The warmth I share with others returns to me and to my baby.
content.hi: Main khaalipan se nahi deti, balki ek shaant bharepan se. Jo garmjoshi main doosron ke saath baant ti hoon woh mujh tak aur mere baby tak lautti hai.

remember.en: Giving from love leaves you fuller, not emptier.
remember.hi: Pyaar se dena aapko zyada bhara chhodta hai, khaali nahi.

babyLearning.en: closing the week, growing calm and ready
babyLearning.hi: hafta poora karte hue, shaant aur taiyaar hote

title.hi: Uplabdhi Se Upar Charitra

insight.en: Long after grades and prizes fade, what truly carries your child is the kind of person they have become.
insight.hi: Number aur inaam dheere bhul jaane ke bahut baad, jo cheez sach mein aapke bachche ko thaame rakhti hai woh hai ki woh kaise insaan bane hain.

expanded.en: It is easy to dream of all your child might achieve — the marks, the medals, the milestones. These are lovely hopes. But the gifts that will hold them steadiest through life are quieter: honesty, kindness, the courage to do right, the warmth to care for others. Achievements can be lost or outgrown, but character stays. A child who knows they are loved for who they are, not only for what they accomplish, grows up grounded and free. As you imagine your baby's future, let your deepest hope be not that they shine brightest, but that they grow good, kind and true. That is a success no report card can measure.
expanded.hi: Apne bachche ki har uplabdhi ka sapna dekhna aasaan hai — number, padak, mukaam. Yeh pyaari ummeedein hain. Par jo tohfe unhe zindagi bhar sabse sthir rakhenge woh zyada shaant hain: imaandaari, dayaalta, sahi karne ki himmat, doosron ki parwah karne ki garmjoshi. Uplabdhiyaan kho sakti hain ya peeche chhoot sakti hain, par charitra rehta hai. Ek bachcha jo jaanta hai ki use uske hone ke liye pyaar kiya jaata hai, sirf uski uplabdhi ke liye nahi, woh jameen se juda aur aazaad bada hota hai. Jaise aap apne baby ke bhavishya ki kalpana karti hain, aapki sabse gehri ummeed yeh na ho ki woh sabse zyada chamke, balki yeh ki woh achhe, dayaalu aur sachche badhein. Yeh ek aisi safalta hai jise koi report card naap nahi sakta.

deepDive.en: Long-term studies on wellbeing find that traits like kindness, honesty and self-control predict a fulfilling life more strongly than achievement alone. Character, not accolades, carries us.
deepDive.hi: Bhalai par hui lambe samay ki studies paati hain ki dayaalta, imaandaari aur self-control jaise gun ek santushti bhari zindagi ko akeli uplabdhi se zyada bharose se bataate hain. Charitra, na ki taareef, humein thaame rakhta hai.

remember.en: Who your child becomes matters more than what they achieve.
remember.hi: Aapka bachcha kaisa insaan banta hai, woh uski uplabdhi se zyada maayne rakhta hai.

title.hi: Udaar Jharna

summary.en: A mountain spring gives its water freely to all and discovers that giving is what keeps it ever flowing.
summary.hi: Ek pahaadi jharna apna paani sabko khulkar deta hai aur paata hai ki dena hi use hamesha behte rehne deta hai.

body.en:
High on a quiet mountainside, a small spring bubbled up from the rock. From the very first, it gave its clear water freely — to the thirsty deer that bent to drink, to the birds that splashed in its shallows, to the little stream that carried its gift down toward the villages below.

A proud boulder nearby scoffed at the spring. "Why give so much away? Hold your water. Keep it safe within the stone, as I keep myself, and you will never run dry." The spring only laughed softly and went on giving, trickling out to all who came.

The seasons turned. The boulder, sealed and unmoving, grew cracked and grey, holding nothing and giving nothing. But the little spring flowed on, year after year, ever fresh — for the more it gave, the more rose up from deep within the earth to take its place. "You see," murmured the spring, "what I pour out is exactly what keeps me alive. A heart that gives is never empty for long."
body.hi:
Ek shaant pahaad ki dhalaan par ooper, ek chhota jharna chattaan se phoot pada. Bilkul shuru se, usne apna saaf paani khulkar diya — pyaase hiran ko jo peene ko jhuka, panchhiyon ko jo uske kinaaron mein chhapaak karte, us chhoti dhaara ko jo uska tohfa niche gaaon ki taraf le jaati.

Paas ki ek ghamandi chattaan ne jharne ka mazaak udaaya. "Itna kyun de dete ho? Apna paani rok lo. Use patthar ke andar surakshit rakho, jaise main khud ko rakhti hoon, aur tum kabhi sookhoge nahi." Jharna bas dheere hansa aur dete rehna jaari rakha, sab aane waalon ke liye behta raha.

Mausam badle. Chattaan, band aur achal, chatakhne aur dhusar pad gayi, kuch na thaame aur kuch na de. Par chhota jharna behta raha, saal-dar-saal, hamesha taaza — kyunki jitna woh deta, utna hi zameen ki gehraai se uthkar uski jagah le leta. "Dekha," jharna phusphusaaya, "jo main bahar undelta hoon wahi mujhe zinda rakhta hai. Ek dil jo deta hai woh zyada der khaali nahi rehta."

title.en: Tell your baby about the character you hope they build, the kind of person you dream they become.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us charitra ke baare mein bataayein jo aap chahti hain woh banaaye, woh kaisa insaan aap sapna dekhti hain woh bane.

motivation.en: Naming the heart you wish for them is the first quiet step in helping it grow.
motivation.hi: Jis dil ki aap unke liye kaamna karti hain use naam dena use badhaane ka pehla shaant kadam hai.

title.en: The Goodness I Wish For You
title.hi: Woh Achhaai Jo Main Tumhare Liye Chahti Hoon

description.en: A short closing meditation on hope and character
description.hi: Ummeed aur charitra par ek chhota samaapan dhyan

affirmation.en: More than anything you will ever do, I wish for the good and gentle person you will be.
affirmation.hi: Tum jo kuch bhi kabhi karoge us se zyada, main us achhe aur naram insaan ki kaamna karti hoon jo tum banoge.

introduction.en: Settle comfortably, close your eyes, and take three slow breaths. Rest a hand on your belly and let your body grow still. Quietly picture the kind of heart you wish for your baby — gentle, honest, brave — and let a warm wish for it flow softly inward.
introduction.hi: Aaram se baithein, aankhein band karein, aur teen dheemi saansein lein. Ek haath pet par rakhein aur apne sharir ko shaant hone dein. Chupchaap us tarah ke dil ki tasveer banaayein jo aap apne baby ke liye chahti hain — naram, imaandaar, himmati — aur uske liye ek garm kaamna ko narmi se andar behne dein.

closingThought.en: You cannot hand your baby their character like a gift. But the love and goodness you live now are already quietly shaping it.
closingThought.hi: Aap apne baby ko unka charitra kisi tohfe ki tarah nahi de sakti. Par jo pyaar aur achhaai aap abhi jeeti hain woh use pehle se chupchaap gadh rahi hai.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Teen Dheemi Saansein

content.en: Breathe in slowly through your nose, feel your chest and belly fill, then release it gently through soft lips. Do just three unhurried rounds and notice the quiet they leave behind.
content.hi: Apni naak se dheere saans lein, apne seene aur pet ko bharte mehsoos karein, phir use naram honthon se pyaar se chhodein. Bas teen bina jaldi ki saansein lein aur woh shaanti note karein jo woh peeche chhod jaati hain.

remember.en: Three calm breaths can reset a whole moment.
remember.hi: Teen shaant saansein ek poore pal ko phir se shaant kar sakti hain.
